title: ranunculus nephrophyllus
intro: Ranunculus nephrophyllus, also known as the bog buttercup or marsh crowfoot, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus nephrophyllus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive issues, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried or made into a tincture, and can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
